name: Druva
description: Cloud-native data protection SaaS with AI-driven autonomous recovery, ransomware detection, and backup automation. Protects endpoints, SaaS apps, cloud infra, and data centers. 250+ telecom operators. SOC2, HIPAA, FedRAMP compliant.
oneLiner: Cloud-native data protection SaaS with AI-driven autonomous recovery, ransomware detection, and backup automation.
pricing: Subscription per workload type. Enterprise contract. No published pricing.
capabilities: Deep Analysis Agents with Agentic Memory for forensic investigations
Identity Resilience for Okta/AD/Entra ID protection
Natural language EC2 workload recovery orchestration

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Not an agent builder. Users configure backup policies via web console. AI surfaces as recommendations, anomaly alerts, and recovery suggestions within existing workflow.
strengths: Single control plane across all workloads
weaknesses: Not an agent factory — AI is embedded, not composable
notableFeatures: Automated DR testing without production impact

recentDevelopments: 2026-02: Deep Analysis Agents with Agentic Memory — forensic investigations from 2-3 days to 8-10 minutes
2026-03: Identity Resilience launched — protects Okta, Microsoft Active Directory, Entra ID in unified SaaS
2026: Image-based assistance — upload screenshots of errors/alerts, DruAI interprets and provides resolution steps
2026: DruAI can orchestrate full EC2 workload recovery (config, volumes, networking) via natural language prompt

deploymentOptions.notes: Cloud-native SaaS with hybrid backup covering on-prem workloads. Control plane is cloud.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Backup validation, recovery testing, detailed logs.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Policy changes on next backup window.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: M365, Google, Salesforce, AWS, Azure, VMware, K8s, NAS, Oracle, SQL.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: No RAG. Backup catalog and metadata queries.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: Pause jobs, halt recovery. Ransomware guardrails.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Tamper-proof logging. SOC2, HIPAA, FedRAMP. Legal hold.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Dashboards for health, storage, compliance. SIEM integration.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: Thousands of enterprise customers. Gartner MQ leader.

scoringRationale: 69/100 overall. Strongest: Operations (17/20). Weakest: Orchestration (7/20). Breakdown: Creation (14/20), Orchestration (7/20), Integration (14/20), Governance (17/20), Operations (17/20).
targetUser: IT teams automating backup, recovery, and ransomware defense

walkthrough.title: AI-Driven Ransomware Detection and Recovery

steps.title: Configure Backup Policies Across Environments
steps.description: Set up unified backup policies for endpoints, SaaS apps (M365, Google Workspace), data centers, and cloud workloads. Druva's SaaS platform requires no infrastructure — policies propagate automatically across your environment.

works: Unified dashboard covers endpoints, data centers, and cloud workloads in one view
Anomaly detection uses ML to flag potential ransomware before damage spreads
Compliance reporting is automated for common frameworks (HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR)
doesntWork: AI features are embedded — not configurable or extensible by users
Dashboard is information-dense — requires training to navigate effectively
Agent deployment to endpoints requires IT admin access and change management
pmCritique.userPsychology: IT admins trust Druva because it is a known entity in data protection — adding AI feels like a natural evolution, not a risky experiment. But the inability to customize AI behavior means it is a black box that either works or does not.

steps.title: Dru AI Detects Anomalous Activity
steps.description: Dru AI continuously monitors backup data for signs of ransomware — unusual encryption patterns, mass file modifications, and data exfiltration indicators. Machine learning models trained on real attack patterns detect threats early.

works: Backup policy configuration is visual and template-driven
SLA monitoring with automated alerting for missed backups
Granular recovery options (file, folder, VM, full system)
doesntWork: Policy creation has too many options for simple use cases — needs progressive disclosure
SLA thresholds are one-size-fits-all — no per-application customization
Recovery testing is manual — no automated DR drill scheduling
pmCritique.userPsychology: Backup admins want set-and-forget reliability — the template system delivers this for standard cases. But the complexity of advanced policy configuration means most organizations underutilize the platform.

steps.title: Autonomous Recovery Orchestration
steps.description: When a threat is confirmed, Dru AI identifies the last clean recovery point and orchestrates granular restoration — recovering only affected files rather than full system restores. Minimizes downtime and data loss.

works: Ransomware detection alerts with clear remediation steps
Quarantine and rollback capabilities are one-click
Forensic analysis tools help understand the attack scope
doesntWork: False positive rate for anomaly detection is not user-configurable
Alert fatigue from non-critical anomalies dilutes attention on real threats
Integration with SIEM/SOAR tools is limited — detection stays siloed in Druva
pmCritique.userPsychology: Ransomware detection creates immediate urgency and justifies the purchase — every IT leader fears being the next headline. But alert fatigue is the silent killer of security tools, and Druva does not solve this.

steps.title: DR Testing with Sandbox Recovery
steps.description: Validate your disaster recovery readiness by spinning up isolated sandbox environments from backup snapshots. Test failover procedures, verify data integrity, and generate compliance reports — all without impacting production.

works: Cloud-native architecture means no on-prem infrastructure to manage
Global deduplication reduces storage costs significantly
API access for automation and integration with IT workflows
doesntWork: Cloud-only model is a dealbreaker for organizations with air-gap requirements
Data egress costs for large recoveries can surprise finance teams
API documentation is adequate but lacks examples for common integration patterns
pmCritique.userPsychology: Cloud-native simplicity is the primary selling point for IT teams drowning in infrastructure management. But the hidden cost of data egress during recovery events — the exact moment when costs should be the last concern — creates resentment.

steps.title: Predictive Optimization
steps.description: Dru AI analyzes backup patterns and storage trends to optimize retention policies, predict capacity needs, and recommend cost-saving storage tier adjustments. Get actionable insights before problems occur.

pmVerdict.summary: Druva is a data protection platform with AI-augmented features, not an agent factory. The AI capabilities (autonomous data recovery, anomaly detection, compliance monitoring) are embedded in the data protection workflow, not exposed as a builder platform. For a PM evaluating agent factories, Druva is a reference implementation of 'AI in a vertical product' rather than a platform to build agents on.
strengths: AI deeply embedded in core workflow — not a bolt-on feature
Data protection domain expertise gives AI strong grounding
risks: Not actually an agent factory — can't build custom agents
AI features are product features, not a platform
No extensibility — can't create new agent types
Vertical-specific — only relevant for data protection use cases

ctoVerdict.summary: Druva demonstrates how AI agents should be embedded in vertical products — autonomous recovery, intelligent tiering, compliance automation. But it's not an agent platform. You can't build agents on Druva. Evaluate as a data protection vendor with AI features, not as an agent factory competitor.
ctoVerdict.decision: NOT APPLICABLE — Vertical Product, Not Agent Platform
strengths: Production-proven AI in data protection — not vaporware
FedRAMP authorized — cleared for government workloads
AI features improve core product — not a separate SKU
dealBreakers: Cannot build custom agents — closed platform
AI features not extensible or configurable by customers
No API for agent creation or orchestration
Relevant only if you need data protection specifically

coverageGaps: Product UI walkthrough (no public demos available)

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: No agent creation capability — AI is embedded, not buildable
scoringAlignment.gap: Score may overstate creation capability — users can't create agents

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: No multi-agent orchestration — single-purpose AI features
scoringAlignment.gap: Orchestration score reflects internal AI coordination, not user-facing
Integration is for data protection, not agent building
Governance is for data protection compliance, not agent governance
Operations score reflects SaaS platform, not agent operations

coreCompetence.bestAt: Enterprise data protection with AI-powered anomaly detection — the most mature cloud backup platform adding agentic AI for ransomware detection and compliance automation.
coreCompetence.targetUser: Enterprise IT and security teams managing data protection across endpoints, data centers, and cloud workloads. Organizations under regulatory compliance pressure.
coreCompetence.vsAlternatives: Wins on data protection domain depth — decades of enterprise backup experience. Wins on compliance automation. Loses — not an agent factory, the AI is embedded in a backup product. Not comparable to general agent platforms.
